epithet: of the Halflings, whose tongue cuts sharper than his blade
distinction_subtitle: the only kill drawn with words alone

archetype_badge.inscription: Boiled leather on his halfling shoulders, a dagger on each hip — somehow the bard became the best-armored soul the party fields.

epithet: of the Wulven, sworn to the hunt
distinction_subtitle: no other hand has thrown so many

archetype_badge.inscription: The hunting trap alone tips twenty-five pounds; above it ride scale mail and a heavy crossbow, and the wulven still finds room for caltrops, rope, and a block-and-tackle besides.

epithet: of the Gold Dragonborn, born to breath and blade
distinction_subtitle: who fells by every means he carries

archetype_badge.inscription: Three tins of salve against the insects, one vial of perfume against the court, and a healing draught for the rest — he does not leave gaps in his preparations.

epithet: of the Rock Gnomes, reader of the threefold weave
distinction_subtitle: the heaviest lift the company has known

archetype_badge.inscription: The book's margins ran out long before the parchment did — eight loose sheets trail it now, dense with whatever the weave kept insisting she hadn't finished writing.

epithet: of the Dhakaani, bound by pact to a shadow unseen
distinction_subtitle: whose dice keep the highest company

archetype_badge.inscription: Fifteen flasks of oil and a lamp that hasn't gone dark yet — a goblin who walked out of Dhakaan carrying enough light to argue with the night.

epithet: of the Tabaxi, mist-pawed and green-eyed in the hunter's hour
distinction_subtitle: alone among rollers without a fumble

archetype_badge.inscription: One carved sprig of mistletoe, ribbon-tied and kept close — the whole of the primal green, if you know how to ask, flows through that small stem.
